title: Knapsack (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the loop bounds are wrong, base the recurrence marks on whether the student applies the recurrence correctly within their own loop.

This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is knapSack(double[] values, int[] volumes, int capacity); the Python function is knapSack(value, volume, capacity). Both are correct.
Both backward (i from n-1 to 0, referencing row i+1) and forward (i from 0 to n-1, referencing row i-1) DP formulations are acceptable.

Items can have a volume of 0. The feasibility check volumes[i] <= j correctly handles zero-weight items for j >= 0; the inner loop must include j = 0 to handle zero-weight items at zero remaining capacity.
Each item can be used at most once (0/1 knapsack).

rubric_points.name: DP table allocation
rubric_points.target: A 2D table of size (n+1) x (capacity+1) is allocated and initialised to 0.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No 2D table is allocated; only a 1D array or no data structure is used to store subproblem results.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A 2D table is present but has wrong dimensions (e.g., n x capacity instead of (n+1) x (capacity+1)), or is not initialised to 0.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: A 2D table of size (n+1) x (capacity+1) is allocated and all cells are initialised to 0.

rubric_points.name: Loop structure: iterating items and capacities
rubric_points.target: An outer loop iterates over all n items and an inner loop iterates j from 0 to capacity inclusive, covering all subproblems including zero-weight items at j = 0.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No nested loops are present; only a single loop or no loop is used.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Nested loops are present but the inner loop starts from j = 1 instead of j = 0 (missing the zero-capacity subproblem needed for weight-0 items), or does not reach capacity, or the outer loop does not cover all n items.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Outer loop iterates over all n items and inner loop iterates j from 0 to capacity inclusive, correctly covering all subproblems including zero-weight items at j = 0.

rubric_points.name: Capacity feasibility check
rubric_points.target: For each item i and capacity j, volumes[i] <= j is checked before deciding to include the item. This correctly handles both regular items and zero-weight items.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No feasibility check is present; the algorithm attempts to include items regardless of whether they fit, causing index errors or incorrect results.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A feasibility check is present but uses strict inequality volumes[i] < j, incorrectly excluding items that exactly fill the remaining capacity, or excluding zero-weight items at j = 0.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: volumes[i] <= j is checked correctly, allowing inclusion when the item fits exactly or with room to spare, and correctly handling zero-weight items.

rubric_points.name: Recurrence relation
rubric_points.target: When item fits: Result[i][j] = max(values[i] + Result[prev][j - volumes[i]], Result[prev][j]). When item does not fit: Result[i][j] = Result[prev][j].

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No recurrence is applied; cells are set to a constant or the values array is used directly without referencing previously computed subproblems.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A max-based recurrence is present but incorrect: only one branch is handled, the wrong row is referenced (current row instead of previous, enabling unlimited item use), or values[i] + Result[prev][j - volumes[i]] is missing the subtraction of volumes[i].

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: When item fits: Result[i][j] = max(values[i] + Result[prev][j - volumes[i]], Result[prev][j]). When item does not fit: Result[i][j] = Result[prev][j]. The previous row is referenced consistently with the chosen loop direction.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: The method returns the correct maximum value for all valid inputs, including items with volume 0.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Method returns 0 or an incorrect value for most inputs.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Returns correct results for standard inputs but fails when items have volume 0, or fails for capacity = 0, or the inner loop starting from j = 1 causes incorrect results for zero-weight items.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns the correct maximum value for all valid inputs including zero-weight items and zero capacity; result is read from the correct cell of the DP table.
